title: pedicularis refracta
intro: Pedicularis refracta, also known as Lousewort, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant, obtaining some of its nutrients by attaching to the roots of other plants. Pedicularis refracta has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to relieve muscle cramps, reduce pain and swelling, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used to treat menstrual cramps, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
